rationale: Keep: the two papers are tightly aligned on online review/WOM evaluation, both modeling when positive vs. negative cues change review value/helpfulness and using large field data plus experiments (citing note: Amazon helpfulness effects; cited note: Yelp usefulness/negativity bias moderation). The cited paper’s core contribution (a moderator and attribution mechanism for positivity discounting) is plausibly a direct theoretical/comparative input to the citing paper’s “when positive emotion enhances vs backfires” question, so this is not an obvious incidental mention.
